query: A One‑Health Framework Translating Metagenomics Research into Clinical and Public‑Health Regulation.

t1.1.name: Metagenomics in One Health Surveillance
t1.1.description: Application of metagenomic sequencing technologies for pathogen detection, antimicrobial resistance monitoring, and ecosystem health assessment across human, animal, and environmental domains.

t1.name: One Health Framework Foundations
t1.description: Conceptual and operational foundations of One Health approaches integrating human, animal, and environmental health through interdisciplinary collaboration and systems thinking.

t2.1.name: Clinical and Public Health Regulation
t2.1.description: Regulatory frameworks, approval pathways, and policy mechanisms governing the clinical validation and public health deployment of metagenomic diagnostic tools and surveillance systems.

t2.name: Translation Challenges and Barriers
t2.description: Technical, institutional, and regulatory obstacles preventing the effective translation of metagenomic research findings into actionable clinical diagnostics and public health interventions.

t3.name: Data Integration and Interoperability
t3.description: Strategies for integrating diverse metagenomic datasets across sectors, ensuring data sharing, standardization, and computational infrastructure for cross-domain One Health analytics.

t1.1.description: Application of metagenomic sequencing technologies for pathogen surveillance, microbiome analysis, and antimicrobial resistance detection across human, animal, and environmental samples within One-Health frameworks.

t1.description: Core principles and conceptual foundations of the One-Health approach, integrating human, animal, and environmental health perspectives in metagenomics research and its translation to policy.

t2.2.description: Integration of metagenomic data into public health surveillance infrastructure for outbreak detection, antimicrobial resistance monitoring, and epidemiological intelligence at local, national, and global scales.

t2.description: Mechanisms, frameworks, and challenges for translating metagenomic research findings into actionable clinical diagnostics, public health policies, and regulatory guidelines.
